violations.text: Observed floor tiles cracked/broken in the (kitchen) area in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2025

The presence of pest is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed on the food-contact surfaces of the slicer. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. -Establishment must decommission slicer to wash, rinse, and sanitize equipment. -Establishment must get pest control company to perform a full evaluation/assessment of facility and provide a plan of action to RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Plan of action must include a treatment plan and a target date to eliminate pest issue. *Plan of action must be submitted to inspector by end of business day 10/7/25. -Establishment to create a daily cleaning checklist and clean the areas on the checklist. Checklist must be provided to inspector upon the next re-inpsection date. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2025
Observed floor tiles cracked/broken in the (kitchen) area in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with a little food debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by several droppings observed in the kitchen on non-food contact surface of prep table. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pest must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pest, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. -Establishment must contact pest control to perform a full assessment/evaluation of the facility and pest control must provide a plan of action detailing the treatment plan and target date for eliminating pests. Establishment shall increase pest control to once a week. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025
Observed 2 door continental lowboy door gaskets were observed in poor repair. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (chlorine) sanitizing solution for the (dishwasher) located in kitchen. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (QT) sanitizing solution for the (3-bay sink) located in bar area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

Observed the non-food contact surfaces of the (stove hood vents) have an accumulation of residue/dust/debris. The floors underneath equipment in kitchen area have an accumulation of residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025
Observed floor tiles cracked/broken in the (kitchen) area in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2025

Raw animal foods (such as ground hamburger) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as deli meats) in the sliding door cooler. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated raw food to lower shelf at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer, knives) were observed with food debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of residue on backplate dripping into ice. PIC cleaned, rinsed and sanitized backplate and voluntarily disposed of ice at time of inspection. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by several droppings observed throughout the kitchen, on shelving, on food-contact surfaces of prep table. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. -Establishment must contact pest control to perform a full assessment/evaluation of the facility and pest control must provide a plan of action detailing the treatment plan and target date for eliminating pests. Establishment shall increase pest control to once a week. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025
Observed 2 door continental lowboy door gaskets were observed in poor repair. Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (QT) sanitizing solution for the (3-bay sink) located in bar area. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (chlorine) sanitizing solution for the (dishwasher) located in kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

Observed the non-food contact surfaces of the (stove hood vents) have an accumulation of residue. The floors underneath equipment in kitchen area have an accumulation of residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025

The outside dumpster is not covered/closed at time of inspection. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered/closed. PIC closed dumpster at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025
Observed floor tiles cracked/broken in the (kitchen) area in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2025
The outdoor walk-in cooler is not in good repair (floors/walls/ceiling in need of repair). Equipment is to be fixed/adjusted and maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
The door to the outdoor walk-in cooler is not in good repair. Equipment is to be fixed/adjusted and maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/08/2024

A drainage line from the ceiling in the office area is draining into a container/bucket. Condensate/liquid drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

The following areas of the physical facilities were in need of repair... - Areas of the kitchen floor tile, tile grout, walls, and floor-to-wall coving was observed to be in poor repair. Per the PIC, currently in the process of replacing/fixing wall coverings and coving that are in need of repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed on food shelving, under/behind kitchen equipment, and in or equipment, and a trapped mouse observed behind the kitchen chest freezer. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC is to ensure... - Pest control to continue weekly treatments/inspections - All food is to continue to be protected from possible adulteration/contamination by pests - Keep the bottom shelving clear from food product - Equipment Surfaces are routinely cleaned and sanitized prior to daily business - Any gaps/entry points in the interior and exterior parts of the facility observed are to be filled or sealed - Outdoor areas between building and equipment are to be decluttered/cleared - Office area is to be decluttered/cleared - Pest Control reports are sent to health inspector for review - Detailed Pest Control Plan of Action is to be sent to inspector for review Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, office, and main entrance areas, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

A drainage line from the ceiling in the office area is draining into a container/bucket. Condensate/liquid drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

The outside areas between equipment and the walls, as well as the inside office area, were observed to have unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items and clutter. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter/clutter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

The following areas of the physical facilities were in need of repair... - Areas of the kitchen floor tile, tile grout, walls, and floor-to-wall coving was observed to be in poor repair. - Outside areas of the building were observed to have possible pest entry points The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2024

The floors and walls under and behind equipment in the facility kitchen, bar, and office were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed on food shelving, under/behind kitchen equipment, and in or equipment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC is to ensure... - Pest control to continue weekly treatments/inspections - All food is to continue to be protected from possible adulteration/contamination by pests - Keep the bottom shelving clear from food product - Equipment Surfaces are routinely cleaned and sanitized prior to daily business - Any gaps/entry points observed are to be filled or sealed - Outdoor areas between building and equipment are to be decluttered/cleared - Pest Control reports are sent to health inspector for review - Detailed Pest Control Plan of Action is to be sent to inspector for review Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024

The exterior walk-in cooler is observed to be in need of repair... - The inner portion of the door was observed to be detached - The base of the door threshold was observed to be in need of adjustment/repair Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving/equipment surfaces in the kitchen area were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and rodent droppings. Per the PIC, more frequent detailed cleaning has been and is to be completed. PIC to continue to clean as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

The following areas of the physical facilities were in need of repair... - Areas of the kitchen floor tile, tile grout, walls, and floor-to-wall coving was observed to be in poor repair. - Outside areas of the building were observed to have possible pest entry points The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

The floors and walls under and behind equipment in the kitchen, dish, storage, and mop sink areas were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and/or droppings. Since previous inspection, per PIC and inspector observation, a more detailed cleaning routine has begun and is to continue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

At the time of inspection, canned goods and a bag of flour was observed to be adulterated by rodent droppings and/or gnawing. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. (Chlorine Sanitizer was replaced with a new bucket at the time of inspection. Chlorine Sanitizer retested at the appropriate level of 50-100ppm at the time of inspection.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

Food contact surfaces of previously cleaned equipment/utensils (deli slicer and knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. (The slicer and knives were taken out of service and set for proper wash/rinse/sanitizing at the time of inspection.) New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed on food containers, droppings observed under/behind equipment, and gnawed food product. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC is to ensure... - Pest control increased to weekly treatments/inspections - All food is protected from possible adulteration/contamination by pests - Equipment Surfaces are routinely cleaned and sanitized prior to daily business - Any gaps/entry points observed are to be filled - Pest Control reports are sent to health inspector for review New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package in the kitchen area. Single service items are to be stored in their original packaging to prevent possible contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

The following equipment was observed to be in need of repair/adjustment... - The Walk-In Cooler door - The cookline prep units were observed to have standing water inside Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observe to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris... - Kitchen refrigeration door tracks, gaskets, and internal/external surfaces - Kitchen shelving/storage racks - Cookline equipment surfaces between equipment Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024

The drain plug for the outside dumpster is missing. Drains in receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must have drain plugs in place. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024

Areas of the kitchen floor tile, tile grout, walls, and floor-to-wall coving was observed to be in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

The floors and walls under and behind equipment in the facility were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, debris, and droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024
